summary: Matt Rocklin reports on collaborative work during the SciPy 2017 sprints exploring how to parallelize scikit-image using Dask. The post is essentially a brief announcement pointing readers to a Jupyter notebook containing the technical details of their investigation. This represents early exploration of integrating Dask's parallel computing capabilities with the popular image processing library.
key_points: Collaborated with scikit-image developers at SciPy 2017 sprints
Investigated parallelizing scikit-image operations using Dask
Work documented in a publicly available Jupyter notebook
money_quotes: This weekend at the SciPy 2017 sprints I worked alongside Scikit-image developers to investigate parallelizing scikit-image with Dask.
key_insight: Dask can potentially be used to parallelize scikit-image operations, as explored during collaborative sprint work at SciPy 2017.
